text.title: rust-testing-antipatterns Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for rust-testing-antipatterns as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Utc::now() in assertion paths. Agents inject the current timestamp inside the unit under test and inside the assertion. Tests pass at noon, fail at midnight when the day boundary crosses.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Use an injected clock (a Clock trait with a mock) or tokio::time::pause to control virtual time. Never compare Utc::now() to Utc::now() in a test assertion.

failure-mode.description: sleep-driven async waits. Agents write tokio::time::sleep(Duration::from_millis(50)) to "give the task time to complete". Flaky in CI under load.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Use tokio::join!, tokio::time::pause + advance, or a channel/oneshot to wait for completion deterministically. If you need to wait for a spawned task, use a JoinHandle.

failure-mode.description: #[tokio::test] flavor mismatch. Default flavor is current_thread. Agents call spawn_blocking or tokio::spawn without specifying flavor = "multi_thread" — the test panics under load because the multi-thread scheduler is not running.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Specify #[tokio::test(flavor = "multi_thread", worker_threads = 2)] when the unit under test spawns tasks or calls spawn_blocking. Run both flavors at least sometimes to surface Send/Sync bugs.

failure-mode.description: Shared lazy_static or once_cell state across test functions without reset. Tests pass locally in isolation, fail under cargo test -- --test-threads=N.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Rust tests run in parallel by default. Global mutable state accumulates across tests. Use a per-test fixture struct or initialize state inside each test function.

failure-mode.description: Hardcoded ports. Integration tests bind 127.0.0.1:8080; CI runs them in parallel and one fails with "address already in use".
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Bind to :0 and discover the port with local_addr(). Or use tower::ServiceExt::oneshot which requires no socket at all.

failure-mode.description: Hallucinated mockall::predicate APIs. Agents invent predicates like predicate::eq_ignoring_case, predicate::starts_with, predicate::matches_regex that do not exist in the current crate version.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Pin the mockall version in Cargo.toml and always verify predicate names against docs.rs/mockall for that version. Use predicate::function(|x| ...) as a fallback for custom logic.

failure-mode.description: unwrap() in test assertions making failures unreadable. The next agent or developer cannot determine which operation failed.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Replace .unwrap() with .expect("description of what should have succeeded"). The message surfaces immediately in the test failure output.

failure-mode.description: Auto-marking flaky tests as #[ignore] without root-cause analysis. Agents flag a flake, mark it ignored, and move on. The hidden bug ships to production.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Never auto-ignore a flake. Quarantine it behind a feature flag or a separate CI job, escalate to a human for triage, and remove the quarantine only after the root cause is fixed.

failure-mode.description: Doctest on private items. Agents add code blocks to private fn rustdoc; they do not run under cargo test --doc.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Promote to a #[test] in the #[cfg(test)] module or to a public example module. Private-item doctests silently provide no coverage.
